# Creative Director (External — Customer)

> External persona — leads a creative team in media & entertainment, advertising, post-production, or in-house content. Buys (or specifies) workstations for editors, animators, VFX artists, and color graders. The contested segment where Apple Silicon has been gaining share.

## Snapshot

- **Title examples:** Creative Director, Director of Post-Production, Head of VFX, Senior Animation Lead, Studio Technical Director.
- **Industry context:** Independent post-production house, ad agency, in-house brand studio, animation studio, VFX boutique, or larger M&E company.
- **Team size:** 4–40 creatives.
- **Tools used 6–10 hours/day:** Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, Photoshop, Illustrator; DaVinci Resolve; Avid Media Composer; Autodesk Maya, 3ds Max; Cinema 4D; Houdini; Nuke; Blender; Pro Tools.
- **Tenure:** 12–25 years in creative production.

## Goals

- Keep the team's creative momentum: no waiting on renders, edits, or color sessions.
- Hit project deadlines — many client-driven, with little buffer.
- Match the workstation to the workflow (a colorist needs different specs than an animator than a video editor).
- Manage capex unpredictably — projects come in waves, and refresh cycles often align with major project starts.
- Stay current with creative software trends (AI-assisted editing, real-time engines, virtual production).

## Frustrations / pains

- Apple Silicon's performance in some creative apps (Resolve, Premiere) has been impressive; team members ask "why aren't we on M-series?"
- Pro GPU supply constraints affect render performance plans.
- Workstations that perform well at launch but slow as software updates push specs.
- Color accuracy and display calibration: workstation choice has to pair with display choice; mistakes here are visible to clients.
- Mobile workstations for on-set use sometimes lag desktop performance significantly.
- Render management at the team level — render farms exist but workstation rendering is still a daily reality for many tasks.

## Buying behavior

- **Decides or co-decides** with studio operations / IT for workstation purchases.
- **Decision criteria (weighted):**
  1. Performance in their specific software stack (timeline scrubbing, render speed, real-time playback).
  2. Display compatibility and color workflow.
  3. Reliability on long renders or edit sessions.
  4. Mobility (for on-set, on-location, client-side work).
  5. Aesthetic and "feel" — creative teams are influenced by the experience of using the device.
  6. Total cost — but less weighted than for IT procurement; performance often wins.
- **Evaluation behaviors:** trial units; benchmarks against current rigs; peer recommendations from forums (LiftGammaGain, Reduser, Adobe forums); YouTube reviews focused on creative workflows.
- **Vendor flexibility:** higher than engineering teams. Will mix Mac and Windows workstations across the team if it serves the work.

## What they want from us

- "Here's the Pro Workstation configuration that benchmarks favorably against MacBook Pro M-series for Resolve / Premiere / Houdini at this price."
- "Here's the validated display + Pro Workstation combination for color-critical work."
- "Here's the AI-augmented creative workflow story — what changes with NPU + RTX vs M-series."
- "Here's the on-set mobile workstation story for [specific use case]."

## What they don't want

- Spec sheets without real-world creative-app benchmarks.
- A workstation that's "good for engineers" assumed to be good for creatives — different bottlenecks.
- Reps who don't know the difference between Premiere and Resolve, or between an animator's and a colorist's workstation needs.
- Mobile workstations that thermal-throttle during long render queues.

## Common objections

- "MacBook Pro M-series is faster for our timeline scrubbing — why Pro Workstation?"
- "Mac Studio is cheaper for the perf we need."
- "Last refresh, the Pro Workstation mobile thermal-throttled on long renders."
- "Color workflow on Windows is more painful than on macOS — we'd need to invest in calibration tooling."
- "We need on-set mobility that matches a 16-inch MacBook Pro."

## Maps to archetypes

- Primary: **Performance-Maximizer** — performance is the ROI.
- Secondary: **Innovator** — adopts new creative tools fast; tolerates rough edges if the capability is there.
